Module 3 -Welcome (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 2
Ry Schwartz: Guys, welcome to Module 3, the segmentation and confirmation phase. Before
we get into this particular module, I want just to make a quick note because this
really is the first of the five modules where we're going to be digging into these
subsequences and exactly how to go about them and really absorb the
information but also apply it right away and really act upon it to create those
emails that go into your funnel or your sequences.
This is how these modules are going to go on from here on out. We're going to
start off with the main lesson video where you're really going to be getting that
full rundown of what we're coaching in this sequence. You're going to get that
deep dive of exactly what this part of your funnel is selling or coaching and
some tips and strategies on how to do that.
Then we're going to hop into the workbooks and you're going to get a rundown
of those major points from the lesson, the really key takeaways that I want to be
super fresh in your mind as you approach crafting your messaging for this.
Then we're going to go over those key questions to ask, the Direct Response 2.0
style, and some of them, most of them, actually will be very familiar from the 47
questions workbook. If you did the work back in Module 2, that's great. You're
going to be prepared to really storm through this aspect of the funnel. If not,
this is where you are going to have to sit down and just answer and explore
these questions. I just don't want you to rush it. Just spend an extra 30 seconds
on it. If you think we're done, just sit in silence with it and see what else comes
up. Be patient. Patience is a competitive advantage sometimes, so just be willing
to explore those questions as fully as you can, and then going even a little bit
beyond that.
Then finally, also in the workbooks, you're going to be getting the themeplates
and the examples. These are going to be the emails I go to, to activate those
things for coaching. You're going to be leveraging a lot of the information you
uncover in those questions and using that to execute these themeplates. But
these themeplates are designed to really be a container for coaching that
specific belief, behavior, action, or trigger that is crucial for that aspect of your
funnel.
That's how we are going to approach Modules 3 through 7, and you're going to
get those themeplate walkthroughs, but there's one thing I want to touch upon
one final time. There's just no such thing as "swipe and deploy". That sounds
like a warfare strategy that you'd hear in Star Trek. It has no place in marketing.
It has no place in connecting with your audience in a genuine way and actually
trying to coach that transformation. We're not swiping and deploying. These are
themeplates. They are loose containers. But they're definitely here to guide
your thinking and just take a lot of the guesswork out of writing these emails.
If there's one thing I've noticed it's that people aren't afraid to roll up their
sleeves, sit down and do the work. They just want that confidence to double
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Module 3 -Welcome (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 2
down on doing it properly. No one wants to be wasting their time. That's what
these containers are designed for, to make you feel like you are putting that
effort and energy and applying it in a way that actually is going to have a pay off.
That's what we're doing here. Not swipe and deploy. That's why you're not
getting, quote, unquote, swipe files and like ad lib, mad lib ... Ad lib or mad lib? I
think it's mad lib. You know, mad lib style things where you just fill in the blank
that connects with absolutely nobody and you probably don't feel good sending
that out. So, no such thing as swipe and deploy, but use these as containers. Use
the guiding questions and then really just kind of let it naturally flow from there.
That's what I'm going to leave you with before we go into Module 3, which is
the segmentation and confirmation. It's really the start of this journey and I
can't wait to see you in the main lesson. So, I'll catch you there in a sec.
